input_params.party_a: Nalin Enterprises (Pvt) Ltd, No. 45, Vauxhall Street, Colombo 02, Company Registration No. PV/2015/04523
input_params.party_b: Chamara Rajapaksha, holder of NIC No. 198834502345, No. 12, Baseline Road, Ratmalana
input_params.contract_purpose: Supply and installation of solar panel system (10kW capacity) at the commercial premises of Party A
input_params.obligations_a: To provide site access, make timely payments as per schedule, and provide written approval for commencement of installation
input_params.obligations_b: To supply, install, test, and commission a 10kW solar panel system, provide a two-year warranty, and complete work within 60 days of signing
input_params.payment_terms: Total contract value Rs. 2,800,000. 30% advance upon signing, 40% upon delivery of materials, 30% upon completion and commissioning

drafted_document:
SOLAR PANEL SUPPLY AND INSTALLATION AGREEMENT

This Agreement is entered into on the 1st day of April 2024,

BETWEEN:

Nalin Enterprises (Pvt) Ltd, a company duly incorporated and registered under the Companies Act, No. 07 of 2007, bearing Company Registration No. PV/2015/04523, having its registered office at No. 45, Vauxhall Street, Colombo 02 (hereinafter referred to as "Party A" or the "Client");

AND:

Chamara Rajapaksha, holder of National Identity Card No. 198834502345, residing at No. 12, Baseline Road, Ratmalana, carrying on business as a solar energy contractor (hereinafter referred to as "Party B" or the "Contractor").

(Party A and Party B are hereinafter collectively referred to as the "Parties" and individually as a "Party".)

RECITALS

A. Party A owns and operates commercial premises at No. 45, Vauxhall Street, Colombo 02, and desires to install a solar panel system of 10kW capacity for the purpose of generating renewable energy.
B. Party B is engaged in the business of supply, installation, and commissioning of solar energy systems and has represented that it has the requisite expertise and resources to undertake the work contemplated herein.
C. The Parties have agreed to enter into this Agreement on the terms and conditions set out herein.

1. DEFINITIONS
   In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires:
   "Works" means the supply, installation, testing, and commissioning of the 10kW solar panel system as more particularly described in Schedule 1 hereto.
   "Site" means the commercial premises of Party A situated at No. 45, Vauxhall Street, Colombo 02.
   "Completion Date" means the 31st day of May 2024.

2. SCOPE OF WORK
   Party B shall supply, deliver, install, test, and commission a solar panel system of 10kW capacity at the Site, in accordance with the specifications set out in Schedule 1 and in compliance with all applicable laws, regulations, and standards in Sri Lanka, including the Sri Lanka Standards applicable to solar energy installations.

3. OBLIGATIONS OF PARTY A
   3.1 Party A shall provide Party B and its authorised personnel with unrestricted access to the Site during normal working hours and at such other times as may be reasonably required for the execution of the Works.
   3.2 Party A shall make all payments strictly in accordance with Clause 5 hereof.
   3.3 Party A shall provide written approval to Party B to commence the Works within seven (07) days of the date of this Agreement.
   3.4 Party A shall not interfere with or impede the execution of the Works.

4. OBLIGATIONS OF PARTY B
   4.1 Party B shall supply all materials, labour, equipment, and tools necessary for the execution of the Works.
   4.2 Party B shall complete the Works by the Completion Date.
   4.3 Party B shall ensure that all Works are executed in a professional, workmanlike manner and in accordance with best industry practices and applicable Sri Lanka Standards.
   4.4 Party B shall provide Party A with a warranty in respect of the Works as set out in Clause 6 hereof.
   4.5 Party B shall obtain all necessary permits and approvals from the Ceylon Electricity Board and other relevant authorities prior to commissioning.

5. PAYMENT TERMS
   5.1 The total contract value for the Works is Rupees Twenty-Eight Lakhs (Rs. 2,800,000/-).
   5.2 Payment shall be made as follows:
       (a) Advance payment of thirty percent (30%), amounting to Rs. 840,000/-, upon execution of this Agreement;
       (b) Second instalment of forty percent (40%), amounting to Rs. 1,120,000/-, upon delivery of all materials to the Site;
       (c) Final payment of thirty percent (30%), amounting to Rs. 840,000/-, upon completion, commissioning, and written acceptance of the Works by Party A.
   5.3 All payments shall be made by way of bank transfer or crossed cheque in favour of Party B.

6. WARRANTY
   Party B warrants that the Works and all materials supplied shall be free from defects in workmanship and materials for a period of two (02) years from the date of commissioning. Party B shall, at its own cost and expense, rectify any defects arising during the warranty period within fourteen (14) days of written notification by Party A.

7. TERMINATION
   7.1 Either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving thirty (30) days' written notice to the other Party in the event of a material breach that remains unremedied after fourteen (14) days of written notice of such breach.
   7.2 In the event of termination by Party A due to the default of Party B, Party A shall be entitled to recover all advance payments made together with damages.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
   Any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall first be referred to mediation in accordance with the Mediation Board Act, No. 72 of 1988. If the dispute is not resolved within thirty (30) days of referral to mediation, either Party may refer the matter to the competent courts of Sri Lanka.

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
   This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka. The Parties irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Colombo, Sri Lanka.

10. ENTIRE AGREEMENT
    This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations, representations, or agreements.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have executed this Agreement on the day and year first above written.

SIGNED by the authorised representative
of NALIN ENTERPRISES (PVT) LTD:

input_params.party_a: Green Leaf Tea Exports (Pvt) Ltd, No. 100, Sir Baron Jayatilaka Mawatha, Colombo 01, Company Registration No. PV/2008/01234
input_params.party_b: Kandy Tea Factory Ltd, No. 5, Factory Road, Peradeniya, Kandy, Company Registration No. PV/2000/00567
input_params.contract_purpose: Purchase and supply of made tea (Orange Pekoe grade) for a period of twelve months
input_params.obligations_a: To purchase a minimum of 5,000 kg of tea per month, make payment within 14 days of invoice, and provide quality specifications in advance
input_params.obligations_b: To supply tea conforming to SLSI quality standards, deliver to Colombo warehouse, and maintain supply continuity
input_params.payment_terms: Price of Rs. 1,200 per kilogram. Payment via bank transfer within 14 days of delivery and invoice. Price subject to quarterly review.
input_params.jurisdiction: Commercial High Court of the Western Province

drafted_document:
TEA SUPPLY AGREEMENT

This Tea Supply Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") is entered into on the 1st day of January 2024,

BETWEEN:

Green Leaf Tea Exports (Pvt) Ltd, a company duly incorporated under the Companies Act, No. 07 of 2007, bearing Company Registration No. PV/2008/01234, having its registered office at No. 100, Sir Baron Jayatilaka Mawatha, Colombo 01 (hereinafter referred to as "Party A" or the "Buyer");

AND:

Kandy Tea Factory Ltd, a company duly incorporated under the Companies Act, No. 07 of 2007, bearing Company Registration No. PV/2000/00567, having its registered office at No. 5, Factory Road, Peradeniya, Kandy (hereinafter referred to as "Party B" or the "Supplier").

RECITALS

A. Party A is engaged in the business of export of Ceylon tea and is desirous of sourcing high-quality made tea on a regular and consistent basis.
B. Party B is engaged in the manufacture of Ceylon tea at its factory located in Peradeniya, Kandy, and has the capacity and resources to supply the quantities required by Party A.
C. The Parties have agreed to enter into this Agreement for the purchase and supply of made tea on the terms and conditions set out herein.

1. DEFINITIONS
   "Made Tea" means Orange Pekoe grade Ceylon black tea manufactured in accordance with the specifications set out in Schedule 1.
   "Sri Lanka Standards" means the standards prescribed by the Sri Lanka Standards Institution (SLSI) for Ceylon tea.
   "Delivery Point" means the warehouse of Party A situated at No. 100, Sir Baron Jayatilaka Mawatha, Colombo 01.

2. SCOPE OF SUPPLY
   Party B shall supply to Party A made tea of Orange Pekoe grade conforming to the Sri Lanka Standards and the specifications set out in Schedule 1, in such quantities as ordered by Party A, subject to a minimum monthly order of five thousand (5,000) kilograms.

3. OBLIGATIONS OF PARTY A
   3.1 Party A shall purchase a minimum of five thousand (5,000) kilograms of Made Tea per calendar month during the term of this Agreement.
   3.2 Party A shall provide Party B with written purchase orders specifying required quantities, quality specifications, and delivery schedules at least seven (07) days prior to the required delivery date.
   3.3 Party A shall make payment in accordance with Clause 5 hereof.

4. OBLIGATIONS OF PARTY B
   4.1 Party B shall supply Made Tea conforming to SLSI quality standards and the specifications set out in Schedule 1.
   4.2 Party B shall deliver the Made Tea to the Delivery Point in good and merchantable condition within the time specified in each purchase order.
   4.3 Party B shall maintain sufficient production capacity to meet Party A's orders and shall give Party A at least thirty (30) days' advance notice of any anticipated supply disruption.
   4.4 Party B shall provide Party B's quality inspection certificates with each delivery.

5. PRICE AND PAYMENT TERMS
   5.1 The price for Made Tea shall be Rupees One Thousand Two Hundred (Rs. 1,200/-) per kilogram, subject to quarterly review as provided in Clause 5.2.
   5.2 The price shall be reviewed at the end of each calendar quarter, and any adjustment shall be agreed upon by the Parties in writing at least fourteen (14) days prior to the commencement of the following quarter.
   5.3 Party A shall make payment by bank transfer to Party B's designated account within fourteen (14) days of delivery and receipt of valid invoice.

6. QUALITY STANDARDS
   All Made Tea supplied under this Agreement shall conform to the standards and specifications prescribed by the SLSI and the Tea Board of Sri Lanka. Party B shall be responsible for the costs of testing and certification. In the event of any dispute regarding quality, an independent tea taster appointed by the Sri Lanka Tea Board shall be the final arbiter.

7. TERMINATION
   Either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving sixty (60) days' written notice. Either Party may terminate immediately upon a material breach by the other that is not remedied within twenty-one (21) days of written notice.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
   Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be referred to arbitration in Colombo under the Arbitration Act, No. 11 of 1995, before a sole arbitrator appointed by mutual agreement or, failing agreement, by the Institute for the Development of Commercial Law and Practice (ICLP).

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
   This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka. The Parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Commercial High Court of the Western Province.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have executed this Agreement on the day and year first above written.

SIGNED for and on behalf of
GREEN LEAF TEA EXPORTS (PVT) LTD:

_________________________
Authorised Signatory
Date: 01.01.2024

SIGNED for and on behalf of
KANDY TEA FACTORY LTD:

_________________________
Authorised Signatory
Date: 01.01.2024

input_params.party_a: Dr. Priyantha Ariyaratne, No. 25, Duplication Road, Colombo 03, NIC No. 197012301234
input_params.party_b: Nirosha Kumari Perera, No. 8, Jawatta Road, Colombo 05, NIC No. 198945604567
input_params.contract_purpose: Employment as personal medical secretary and office administrator at the private medical clinic of Party A
input_params.obligations_a: To pay monthly salary, provide statutory benefits, maintain safe working environment, and give one month notice before termination
input_params.obligations_b: To perform secretarial and administrative duties, maintain patient confidentiality, work office hours Monday to Saturday, and give one month notice before resignation
input_params.payment_terms: Monthly gross salary of Rs. 75,000 payable on the last working day of each month, with EPF at 8% employee contribution and 12% employer contribution, and ETF at 3%

drafted_document:
CONTRACT OF EMPLOYMENT

This Contract of Employment (hereinafter referred to as the "Contract") is entered into on the 1st day of March 2024,

BETWEEN:

Dr. Priyantha Ariyaratne, holder of National Identity Card No. 197012301234, residing at No. 25, Duplication Road, Colombo 03, carrying on practice as a medical practitioner (hereinafter referred to as the "Employer" or "Party A");

AND:

Nirosha Kumari Perera, holder of National Identity Card No. 198945604567, residing at No. 8, Jawatta Road, Colombo 05 (hereinafter referred to as the "Employee" or "Party B").

1. APPOINTMENT AND DURATION
   1.1 The Employer hereby employs the Employee in the capacity of Personal Medical Secretary and Office Administrator at the Employer's private medical clinic.
   1.2 The employment shall commence on the 1st day of March 2024 and shall continue until the 28th day of February 2026, unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions of this Contract.

2. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
   2.1 The Employee shall perform all duties ordinarily associated with the position of Personal Medical Secretary and Office Administrator, including but not limited to:
       (a) Managing patient appointments and medical records;
       (b) Handling correspondence, billing, and administrative paperwork;
       (c) Receiving and directing patients and visitors;
       (d) Maintaining the medical clinic premises in a neat and orderly manner;
       (e) Such other duties as the Employer may reasonably assign from time to time.
   2.2 The Employee shall work from Monday to Saturday during normal clinic hours, being 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., subject to such variations as may be directed by the Employer.

3. OBLIGATIONS OF THE EMPLOYER
   3.1 The Employer shall pay the Employee the monthly salary as stipulated in Clause 4 hereof.
   3.2 The Employer shall make all statutory contributions including EPF and ETF in accordance with the Employees' Provident Fund Act, No. 15 of 1958, and the Employees' Trust Fund Act, No. 46 of 1980.
   3.3 The Employer shall maintain a safe and healthy working environment in compliance with applicable occupational health and safety standards.
   3.4 The Employer shall give the Employee not less than one (01) calendar month's written notice prior to termination of employment, except in cases of summary dismissal for cause.

4. OBLIGATIONS OF THE EMPLOYEE
   4.1 The Employee shall diligently, honestly, and faithfully perform all duties assigned by the Employer.
   4.2 The Employee shall maintain the strictest confidentiality regarding all patient information, medical records, and clinical data, in accordance with the provisions of the Health Services Act and professional ethical standards.
   4.3 The Employee shall give the Employer not less than one (01) calendar month's written notice prior to resignation.
   4.4 The Employee shall not engage in any employment, business, or activity that conflicts with the interests of the Employer during the currency of this Contract.

5. REMUNERATION AND BENEFITS
   5.1 The Employer shall pay the Employee a monthly gross salary of Rupees Seventy-Five Thousand (Rs. 75,000/-), payable on the last working day of each calendar month.
   5.2 The Employer shall deduct and remit the Employee's contribution to the Employees' Provident Fund at the rate of eight percent (8%) of the Employee's monthly gross salary, and shall make the employer's contribution at the rate of twelve percent (12%).
   5.3 The Employer shall make the employer's contribution to the Employees' Trust Fund at the rate of three percent (3%) of the Employee's monthly gross salary.
   5.4 The Employee shall be entitled to such leave as prescribed under the Shop and Office Employees (Regulation of Employment and Remuneration) Act, No. 19 of 1954.

6. CONFIDENTIALITY
   The Employee shall, both during and after the term of this Contract, maintain complete confidentiality with respect to all patient medical records, clinical information, business affairs of the Employer, and any other information designated as confidential. The Employee shall not disclose any such information to any third party without the prior written consent of the Employer.

7. TERMINATION
   7.1 Either Party may terminate this Contract by giving one (01) month's written notice to the other.
   7.2 The Employer may summarily dismiss the Employee without notice in cases of gross misconduct, dishonesty, wilful neglect of duty, or breach of patient confidentiality.
   7.3 Any dispute arising out of the termination of this Contract shall be referred to the Labour Tribunal of Colombo in accordance with the Industrial Disputes Act, No. 43 of 1950.

8. GOVERNING LAW
   This Contract shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka, including the Shop and Office Employees (Regulation of Employment and Remuneration) Act, No. 19 of 1954, the Industrial Disputes Act, No. 43 of 1950, and all other applicable labour legislation.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have executed this Contract on the day and year first above written.

SIGNED by the EMPLOYER:
Dr. Priyantha Ariyaratne

_________________________
Date: 01.03.2024

SIGNED by the EMPLOYEE:
Nirosha Kumari Perera

_________________________
Date: 01.03.2024

WITNESS:
_________________________
Name:
NIC No.:

input_params.party_b: Dilshan Event Management Services, No. 34, Thurstan Road, Colombo 03, Business Registration No. BN/2016/03456
input_params.contract_purpose: Event management and catering services for the Annual Corporate Gala Dinner of Party A to be held on 15th June 2024
input_params.obligations_a: To provide the event venue, pay agreed fees on schedule, provide a guest list of 300 persons, and designate a liaison officer
input_params.obligations_b: To plan, organise and execute all aspects of the gala dinner including decor, catering for 300 guests, entertainment, photography, and audio-visual setup
input_params.payment_terms: Total fee of Rs. 4,500,000. 25% upon signing, 50% seven days before the event, 25% within 7 days after the event
input_params.jurisdiction: Commercial High Court of the Western Province

drafted_document:
EVENT MANAGEMENT AND CATERING SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Agreement is entered into on the 1st day of May 2024,

BETWEEN:

Lanka Hotels (Pvt) Ltd, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, No. 07 of 2007, bearing Company Registration No. PV/1999/00891, having its registered office at No. 10, Galle Face Court 2, Colombo 03 (hereinafter referred to as "Party A" or the "Client");

AND:

Dilshan Event Management Services, a business entity registered under the Business Names (Registration) Act, bearing Business Registration No. BN/2016/03456, having its principal place of business at No. 34, Thurstan Road, Colombo 03 (hereinafter referred to as "Party B" or the "Event Manager").

RECITALS

A. Party A desires to organise an Annual Corporate Gala Dinner for approximately three hundred (300) guests to be held on the 15th day of June 2024 at the premises of Party A.
B. Party B is engaged in the provision of event management and catering services and has the expertise, resources, and capacity to provide such services.
C. Party A has engaged Party B, and Party B has agreed to provide the services, on the terms and conditions set out herein.

1. SCOPE OF SERVICES
   Party B shall plan, organise, manage, and execute the Annual Corporate Gala Dinner of Party A on the 15th day of June 2024, including the following services:
   (a) Conceptualisation and implementation of event theme and decor;
   (b) Catering services comprising a full sit-down dinner and beverages for three hundred (300) guests;
   (c) Arrangement and management of live entertainment;
   (d) Professional photography and videography;
   (e) Audio-visual equipment setup and technical support;
   (f) Event co-ordination and management on the day of the event.

2. OBLIGATIONS OF PARTY A
   2.1 Party A shall provide the venue at its premises for the Gala Dinner at no additional charge to Party B.
   2.2 Party A shall provide Party B with a confirmed guest list of three hundred (300) persons not later than the 1st day of June 2024.
   2.3 Party A shall designate a liaison officer to co-ordinate with Party B throughout the planning and execution of the event.
   2.4 Party A shall make all payments strictly in accordance with Clause 4 hereof.

3. OBLIGATIONS OF PARTY B
   3.1 Party B shall provide all services set out in Clause 1 to the highest professional standard.
   3.2 Party B shall submit an event plan and menu proposal to Party A for approval by the 15th day of May 2024.
   3.3 Party B shall ensure all food and beverages comply with the applicable food safety regulations under the Food Act, No. 26 of 1980.
   3.4 Party B shall ensure all necessary permits for entertainment and catering are obtained prior to the event.

4. PAYMENT TERMS
   4.1 The total fee for the services is Rupees Forty-Five Lakhs (Rs. 4,500,000/-).
   4.2 Payment shall be made as follows:
       (a) Twenty-five percent (25%), amounting to Rs. 1,125,000/-, upon execution of this Agreement;
       (b) Fifty percent (50%), amounting to Rs. 2,250,000/-, seven (07) days before the date of the event;
       (c) Balance twenty-five percent (25%), amounting to Rs. 1,125,000/-, within seven (07) days after the event.

5. CANCELLATION POLICY
   5.1 If Party A cancels the event with less than thirty (30) days' notice, Party A shall forfeit the advance payment.
   5.2 If Party B fails to deliver the agreed services, Party A shall be entitled to a full refund of all payments made, together with damages.

6. LIABILITY
   Party B shall indemnify and hold harmless Party A from any claims, losses, or damages arising from the negligence or wilful misconduct of Party B or its staff during the event.

7. TERMINATION
   Either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving fourteen (14) days' written notice in the event of a material breach. Obligations relating to payments already due shall survive termination.

8. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
   This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka. The Parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Commercial High Court of the Western Province.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have executed this Agreement on the day and year first above written.

SIGNED for and on behalf of
LANKA HOTELS (PVT) LTD:

_________________________
Authorised Signatory
Date: 01.05.2024

SIGNED for and on behalf of
DILSHAN EVENT MANAGEMENT SERVICES:

_________________________
Authorised Signatory
Date: 01.05.2024

input_params.party_a: Harsha Kumara Dissanayake, NIC No. 198323402345, No. 17, Pirivena Road, Kelaniya
input_params.party_b: Manoj Priyashantha Fernando, NIC No. 198734501234, No. 5, New Road, Wattala
input_params.contract_purpose: Lease of residential premises at No. 17, Pirivena Road, Kelaniya for residential use only
input_params.obligations_a: To ensure quiet enjoyment of the premises, maintain structural integrity of the property, and pay all municipal rates and taxes
input_params.obligations_b: To pay rent punctually, maintain the premises in good order, not sublease, and surrender possession at the end of the tenancy
input_params.payment_terms: Monthly rent of Rs. 45,000 payable on or before the 5th of each month. Refundable security deposit of Rs. 90,000 (two months' rent) payable upon signing.

drafted_document:
RESIDENTIAL LEASE AGREEMENT

This Residential Lease Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") is entered into on the 1st day of February 2024,

BETWEEN:

Harsha Kumara Dissanayake, holder of National Identity Card No. 198323402345, residing at No. 17, Pirivena Road, Kelaniya, Western Province (hereinafter referred to as the "Lessor" or "Party A");

AND:

Manoj Priyashantha Fernando, holder of National Identity Card No. 198734501234, residing at No. 5, New Road, Wattala, Western Province (hereinafter referred to as the "Lessee" or "Party B").

1. DESCRIPTION OF PREMISES
   The Lessor hereby leases to the Lessee the residential premises comprising a single-storey house situate at No. 17, Pirivena Road, Kelaniya (hereinafter referred to as the "Premises"), together with all fixtures, fittings, and appurtenances thereto.

2. TERM OF LEASE
   The tenancy shall commence on the 1st day of February 2024 and shall expire on the 31st day of January 2026, being a period of two (02) years, unless sooner determined in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and the Rent Act, No. 07 of 1972.

3. RENT AND SECURITY DEPOSIT
   3.1 The monthly rent for the Premises shall be Rupees Forty-Five Thousand (Rs. 45,000/-), payable on or before the 5th day of each calendar month.
   3.2 The Lessee shall, upon the execution of this Agreement, pay to the Lessor a refundable security deposit of Rupees Ninety Thousand (Rs. 90,000/-) equivalent to two months' rent, which shall be held by the Lessor as security for the due performance by the Lessee of all obligations under this Agreement.
   3.3 The security deposit shall be refunded to the Lessee within thirty (30) days of the expiry or earlier termination of this Agreement, less any deductions for outstanding rent or damage to the Premises caused by the Lessee.

4. OBLIGATIONS OF THE LESSOR
   4.1 The Lessor shall ensure the Lessee's quiet enjoyment of the Premises throughout the term of this Agreement.
   4.2 The Lessor shall maintain the structural integrity of the Premises and attend to major structural repairs.
   4.3 The Lessor shall pay all municipal rates, taxes, and levies assessed upon the Premises.

5. OBLIGATIONS OF THE LESSEE
   5.1 The Lessee shall pay rent strictly in accordance with Clause 3 hereof.
   5.2 The Lessee shall keep and maintain the Premises and all fixtures and fittings in good, clean, and tenantable condition, reasonable wear and tear excepted.
   5.3 The Lessee shall not sublease, assign, or otherwise part with possession of the Premises or any part thereof without the prior written consent of the Lessor.
   5.4 The Lessee shall not carry out any structural alterations to the Premises without the prior written consent of the Lessor.
   5.5 The Lessee shall vacate and surrender possession of the Premises in good condition at the expiry of the tenancy.
   5.6 The Lessee shall pay all electricity, water, and telephone bills relating to the Premises.

6. PERMITTED USE
   The Lessee shall use the Premises for residential purposes only and shall not carry on any trade, business, or profession from the Premises without the prior written consent of the Lessor.

7. TERMINATION
   7.1 This Agreement may be terminated by either Party giving two (02) calendar months' written notice.
   7.2 The Lessor may terminate this Agreement and recover possession in the event of default in the payment of rent for two (02) consecutive months or a material breach of the obligations herein, subject to the provisions of the Rent Act, No. 07 of 1972.

8. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
   This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Sri Lanka, including the Rent Act, No. 07 of 1972. Any dispute shall be referred to the Magistrate's Court of Gampaha.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have executed this Agreement on the day and year first above written.

SIGNED by the LESSOR:
Harsha Kumara Dissanayake

_________________________
Date: 01.02.2024

SIGNED by the LESSEE:
Manoj Priyashantha Fernando

_________________________
Date: 01.02.2024

WITNESS:
_________________________
Name:
NIC No.:

input_params.party_a: National Hospital of Sri Lanka, Regent Street, Colombo 10, represented by the Director General of Health Services
input_params.party_b: MediPharm Supplies (Pvt) Ltd, No. 56, Vauxhall Lane, Colombo 02, Company Registration No. PV/2010/02345
input_params.contract_purpose: Supply of essential medicines and pharmaceutical supplies to the National Hospital of Sri Lanka
input_params.obligations_a: To issue timely purchase orders, provide storage facilities, inspect and accept deliveries, and make payments as per terms
input_params.obligations_b: To supply medicines meeting MOH specifications, deliver within stipulated timeframes, maintain cold chain where necessary, and provide certificates of analysis
input_params.payment_terms: Payment within 60 days of delivery and invoice, in accordance with Government Financial Regulations. Prices as per the approved tender schedule.
input_params.jurisdiction: High Court of the Western Province

drafted_document:
PHARMACEUTICAL SUPPLY AGREEMENT

This Pharmaceutical Supply Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") is entered into on the 1st day of January 2024,

BETWEEN:

The National Hospital of Sri Lanka, Regent Street, Colombo 10, a government institution established under the Ministry of Health, represented by the Director General of Health Services (hereinafter referred to as "Party A" or the "Purchaser");

AND:

MediPharm Supplies (Pvt) Ltd, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, No. 07 of 2007, bearing Company Registration No. PV/2010/02345, having its registered office at No. 56, Vauxhall Lane, Colombo 02 (hereinafter referred to as "Party B" or the "Supplier").

RECITALS

A. Party A is a principal government hospital requiring a regular and uninterrupted supply of essential medicines and pharmaceutical supplies for the treatment of patients.
B. Party B is engaged in the importation and supply of pharmaceutical products and has been awarded this contract following a competitive tender process conducted in accordance with the Government Procurement Guidelines.
C. The Parties have agreed to execute this Agreement on the terms and conditions set out herein.

1. DEFINITIONS
   "Medicines" means the pharmaceutical products listed in the approved Tender Schedule (Schedule 1) which forms part of this Agreement.
   "MOH Specifications" means the specifications for pharmaceutical products prescribed by the Ministry of Health of Sri Lanka.
   "Government Financial Regulations" means the Financial Regulations of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka.

2. SCOPE OF SUPPLY
   Party B shall supply to Party A the Medicines set out in the Tender Schedule (Schedule 1) in such quantities as ordered by Party A from time to time during the term of this Agreement.

3. OBLIGATIONS OF PARTY A
   3.1 Party A shall issue written purchase orders specifying item descriptions, quantities, and required delivery dates.
   3.2 Party A shall provide adequate storage facilities including cold storage where required.
   3.3 Party A shall inspect and accept or reject deliveries within seven (07) days of delivery.
   3.4 Party A shall make payments in accordance with Clause 5 hereof.

4. OBLIGATIONS OF PARTY B
   4.1 Party B shall supply Medicines conforming strictly to the MOH Specifications and the requirements of the National Medicines Regulatory Authority (NMRA) of Sri Lanka.
   4.2 Party B shall deliver the Medicines within the timeframes stipulated in each purchase order.
   4.3 Party B shall maintain the required cold chain for temperature-sensitive pharmaceutical products from the point of manufacture to the point of delivery.
   4.4 Party B shall provide Certificates of Analysis and relevant regulatory approvals for each batch of Medicines delivered.
   4.5 Party B shall comply with all applicable provisions of the Cosmetics, Devices and Drugs Act, No. 27 of 1980.

5. PRICE AND PAYMENT
   5.1 The prices for the Medicines shall be as set out in the approved Tender Schedule (Schedule 1).
   5.2 Party A shall make payment within sixty (60) days of delivery and receipt of a valid invoice, in accordance with the Government Financial Regulations.

6. QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE
   All Medicines supplied shall bear valid NMRA registration, shall be within their shelf life, and shall comply with all applicable laws and regulations of Sri Lanka. Non-conforming goods shall be rejected and returned at Party B's cost.

7. TERMINATION
   Party A may terminate this Agreement by giving thirty (30) days' written notice or immediately in the event of a serious breach relating to quality, safety, or regulatory compliance.

8. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
   This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka. Any dispute shall be subject to the jurisdiction of the High Court of the Western Province.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have executed this Agreement on the day and year first above written.

SIGNED for and on behalf of
NATIONAL HOSPITAL OF SRI LANKA:

_________________________
Director General of Health Services
Date: 01.01.2024

SIGNED for and on behalf of
MEDIPHARM SUPPLIES (PVT) LTD:

_________________________
Authorised Signatory
Date: 01.01.2024

input_params.party_a: Colombo International School (Pvt) Ltd, No. 28, Gregory's Road, Colombo 07, Company Registration No. PV/2005/01122
input_params.party_b: Ms. Renuka Sewwandi Samarasekara, NIC No. 199145602345, No. 9, Bullers Lane, Colombo 07
input_params.contract_purpose: Appointment as a full-time secondary school teacher in the Mathematics department
input_params.obligations_a: To pay monthly salary, provide professional development opportunities, maintain a safe working environment, and comply with all employment laws
input_params.obligations_b: To teach assigned classes, prepare lesson plans, invigilate examinations, participate in school activities, and maintain professional conduct
input_params.payment_terms: Monthly gross salary of Rs. 120,000, payable on the last working day of each month, with statutory EPF (8% employee, 12% employer) and ETF (3%) contributions

drafted_document:
TEACHER EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT

This Contract of Employment (hereinafter referred to as the "Contract") is entered into on the 15th day of January 2024,

BETWEEN:

Colombo International School (Pvt) Ltd, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, No. 07 of 2007, bearing Company Registration No. PV/2005/01122, having its registered office at No. 28, Gregory's Road, Colombo 07 (hereinafter referred to as the "School" or "Party A");

AND:

Ms. Renuka Sewwandi Samarasekara, holder of National Identity Card No. 199145602345, residing at No. 9, Bullers Lane, Colombo 07 (hereinafter referred to as the "Teacher" or "Party B").

1. APPOINTMENT AND DURATION
   1.1 The School hereby appoints the Teacher as a full-time Secondary School Teacher in the Mathematics Department with effect from the 15th day of January 2024.
   1.2 This Contract shall remain in force until the 31st day of December 2025, unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof.

2. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
   2.1 The Teacher shall:
       (a) Teach Mathematics to assigned secondary school classes in accordance with the prescribed curriculum;
       (b) Prepare lesson plans, teaching materials, and assessments;
       (c) Maintain accurate records of student attendance, progress, and performance;
       (d) Invigilate internal and external examinations as assigned;
       (e) Participate in parent-teacher meetings, school events, and professional development activities;
       (f) Perform such other duties as may be assigned by the Principal from time to time.
   2.2 The Teacher shall adhere to the working hours of the School, being Monday to Friday, from 7:30 a.m. to 3:30 p.m., and on Saturdays as required.

3. OBLIGATIONS OF THE SCHOOL
   3.1 The School shall pay the Teacher in accordance with Clause 4 hereof.
   3.2 The School shall provide a safe, supportive, and professional working environment.
   3.3 The School shall provide access to professional development opportunities and training.
   3.4 The School shall comply with all applicable provisions of the Shop and Office Employees (Regulation of Employment and Remuneration) Act, No. 19 of 1954, and all other applicable employment legislation.
   3.5 The School shall give the Teacher not less than one (01) month's written notice prior to termination, except in cases of summary dismissal.

4. OBLIGATIONS OF THE TEACHER
   4.1 The Teacher shall diligently and professionally perform all duties assigned.
   4.2 The Teacher shall maintain the highest standards of professional conduct and shall comply with the School's policies and codes of conduct.
   4.3 The Teacher shall maintain the confidentiality of student records and school administrative matters.
   4.4 The Teacher shall give the School not less than one (01) month's written notice prior to resignation.

5. REMUNERATION AND BENEFITS
   5.1 The School shall pay the Teacher a monthly gross salary of Rupees One Hundred and Twenty Thousand (Rs. 120,000/-), on the last working day of each month.
   5.2 The School shall deduct and remit the Teacher's EPF contribution at eight percent (8%) of the gross salary, and shall contribute twelve percent (12%) as the employer's EPF contribution.
   5.3 The School shall contribute three percent (3%) of the gross salary to the Employees' Trust Fund.
   5.4 The Teacher shall be entitled to leave as prescribed under applicable legislation.

6. CODE OF CONDUCT
   The Teacher shall at all times maintain professional conduct both within and outside the School, uphold the reputation of the School, and shall not engage in any activity that could bring disrepute to the School or be detrimental to the welfare of students.

7. TERMINATION
   7.1 Either Party may terminate this Contract by giving one (01) calendar month's written notice.
   7.2 The School may summarily terminate this Contract in the event of gross misconduct, dishonesty, or conduct prejudicial to the welfare of students, subject to due process under the Industrial Disputes Act, No. 43 of 1950.

8. GOVERNING LAW
   This Contract shall be governed by the laws of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka. Any dispute shall be referred to the Labour Tribunal of Colombo.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have executed this Contract on the day and year first above written.

SIGNED for and on behalf of
COLOMBO INTERNATIONAL SCHOOL (PVT) LTD:

_________________________
Principal / Authorised Signatory
Date: 15.01.2024

SIGNED by the TEACHER:
Ms. Renuka Sewwandi Samarasekara

_________________________
Date: 15.01.2024

input_params.party_b: Asela Roshan Gunasinghe, NIC No. 198534506789, No. 22, Park Road, Boralesgamuwa

drafted_document:
PERSONAL LOAN AGREEMENT

This Personal Loan Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") is entered into on the 15th day of March 2024,

BETWEEN:

Sampath Bank PLC, a licensed commercial bank incorporated under the Companies Act and licensed under the Banking Act, No. 30 of 1988, bearing Company Registration No. PQ/1987/00012, having its registered head office at No. 110, Sir James Peiris Mawatha, Colombo 02 (hereinafter referred to as the "Bank" or "Party A");

AND:

Asela Roshan Gunasinghe, holder of National Identity Card No. 198534506789, residing at No. 22, Park Road, Boralesgamuwa, Western Province (hereinafter referred to as the "Borrower" or "Party B").

1. LOAN AMOUNT AND PURPOSE
   1.1 The Bank hereby agrees to grant to the Borrower a personal loan of Rupees Twenty Lakhs (Rs. 2,000,000/-) (hereinafter referred to as the "Loan Amount").
   1.2 The Loan Amount shall be utilised solely and exclusively for the purpose of renovation of the Borrower's residential property at No. 22, Park Road, Boralesgamuwa.

2. DISBURSEMENT
   The Bank shall disburse the Loan Amount to the Borrower's savings account No. xxxxxxxxxx maintained with Sampath Bank PLC within seven (07) business days of execution of this Agreement and fulfilment of all conditions precedent.

3. INTEREST RATE
   The Loan Amount shall bear interest at the rate of fourteen percent (14%) per annum on a reducing balance basis, calculated monthly on the outstanding principal balance.

4. REPAYMENT TERMS
   4.1 The Loan Amount together with interest shall be repaid by the Borrower in sixty (60) equal monthly instalments of approximately Rupees Forty-Six Thousand Five Hundred and Thirty-Five (Rs. 46,535/-) each.
   4.2 Repayment shall commence on the 1st day of April 2024 and continue on the 1st day of each succeeding month until all outstanding amounts are fully settled.
   4.3 The Borrower may make prepayment of the Loan in whole or in part, subject to the prepayment terms of the Bank prevailing at the time.

5. SECURITY
   As security for the due repayment of the Loan, the Borrower shall execute a Mortgage over the property at No. 22, Park Road, Boralesgamuwa, in favour of the Bank, in such form as the Bank may require.

6. OBLIGATIONS OF THE BANK
   6.1 The Bank shall disburse the Loan Amount in accordance with Clause 2.
   6.2 The Bank shall maintain proper accounts of all transactions relating to the Loan.
   6.3 The Bank shall provide the Borrower with monthly statements of the Loan account.
   6.4 The Bank shall not alter the terms of the Loan without the prior written consent of the Borrower.

7. OBLIGATIONS OF THE BORROWER
   7.1 The Borrower shall repay all monthly instalments strictly on the due dates.
   7.2 The Borrower shall utilise the Loan Amount only for the purpose stated in Clause 1.2.
   7.3 The Borrower shall execute all security documents required by the Bank.
   7.4 The Borrower shall notify the Bank in writing of any change in address or employment within fourteen (14) days of such change.

8. EVENTS OF DEFAULT
   The entire outstanding balance shall become immediately due and payable upon: (a) failure to pay any instalment within thirty (30) days of the due date; (b) insolvency of the Borrower; or (c) any material breach of this Agreement.

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
   This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka and the regulations of the Central Bank of Sri Lanka. Any dispute shall be subject to the jurisdiction of the Commercial High Court of the Western Province.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have executed this Agreement on the day and year first above written.

SIGNED for and on behalf of SAMPATH BANK PLC:

_________________________
Authorised Officer
Date: 15.03.2024

SIGNED by the BORROWER:
Asela Roshan Gunasinghe

_________________________
Date: 15.03.2024

WITNESS:
_________________________
Name:
NIC No.:

drafted_document:
SOFTWARE DEVELOPMENT AND IT CONSULTANCY AGREEMENT

This Software Development and IT Consultancy Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") is entered into on the 1st day of June 2024,

BETWEEN:

Seylan Merchant Bank Ltd, a company incorporated under the Companies Act and licensed under the Banking Act, No. 30 of 1988, bearing Company Registration No. PQ/2001/00456, having its registered office at No. 90, Galle Road, Colombo 03 (hereinafter referred to as "Party A" or the "Client");

AND:

Technosoft Solutions (Pvt) Ltd, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, No. 07 of 2007, bearing Company Registration No. PV/2018/05678, having its registered office at No. 34, Dharmapala Mawatha, Colombo 03 (hereinafter referred to as "Party B" or the "Developer").

RECITALS

A. Party A desires to enhance its banking operations through the development of a custom core banking system module.
B. Party B is engaged in the provision of software development and IT consultancy services and has the requisite technical expertise.
C. Party A has engaged Party B to develop the said software module on the terms and conditions set out herein.

1. SCOPE OF SERVICES
   Party B shall design, develop, test, and implement a core banking system module for Party A, as more particularly described in the Technical Specification Document (Schedule 1), including all integrations with Party A's existing systems.

2. DELIVERABLES AND MILESTONES
   2.1 System Requirements Specification (SRS): within 30 days of commencement.
   2.2 Prototype / Working Model: within 150 days of commencement.
   2.3 User Acceptance Testing (UAT) ready version: within 270 days of commencement.
   2.4 Go-Live and full deployment: by 31st May 2025.
   2.5 Post-implementation support for a period of six (06) months from go-live date.

3. OBLIGATIONS OF PARTY A
   3.1 Party A shall provide clear, complete, and timely requirements to Party B.
   3.2 Party A shall provide timely written feedback on all deliverables within seven (07) business days.
   3.3 Party A shall provide Party B with access to its test environment and such data as may be necessary.
   3.4 Party A shall make all payments in accordance with Clause 5 hereof.

4. OBLIGATIONS OF PARTY B
   4.1 Party B shall develop and deliver the software module in accordance with the Technical Specification and milestones in Clause 2.
   4.2 Party B shall provide all source codes, documentation, and technical manuals upon completion.
   4.3 Party B shall conduct comprehensive user training for Party A's staff prior to go-live.
   4.4 Party B shall provide six (06) months of post-implementation support.
   4.5 Party B shall comply with the data security and confidentiality requirements of the Central Bank of Sri Lanka.

5. PAYMENT TERMS
   5.1 The total contract value is Rupees One Crore Eighty Lakhs (Rs. 18,000,000/-).
   5.2 Payments shall be made as follows:
       (a) 20% (Rs. 3,600,000/-) upon execution of this Agreement;
       (b) 30% (Rs. 5,400,000/-) upon delivery and acceptance of the prototype;
       (c) 30% (Rs. 5,400,000/-) upon successful completion of UAT;
       (d) 20% (Rs. 3,600,000/-) upon go-live.

6. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
   All intellectual property rights in the software module and related deliverables developed under this Agreement shall vest exclusively in Party A upon full payment of the contract value. Party B shall execute all documents necessary to give effect to this transfer of intellectual property.

7. CONFIDENTIALITY
   Party B shall maintain strict confidentiality regarding all information, data, and systems of Party A and its customers. This obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement.

8. TERMINATION
   Either Party may terminate by giving sixty (60) days' written notice. Party A may terminate immediately in the event of a security breach or material failure to deliver.

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
   This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka. Any dispute shall be subject to the jurisdiction of the Commercial High Court of the Western Province.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have executed this Agreement on the day and year first above written.

SIGNED for and on behalf of SEYLAN MERCHANT BANK LTD:

_________________________
Authorised Signatory
Date: 01.06.2024

SIGNED for and on behalf of TECHNOSOFT SOLUTIONS (PVT) LTD:

_________________________
Authorised Signatory
Date: 01.06.2024
